title: A teacher I remember from primary school
summary: Narrate one specific memory of a teacher from your primary school years.
topic: A teacher I remember from primary school
prompt: Write 150–250 words about one teacher you remember from primary school. Focus on ONE specific memory. Use past simple and past continuous. Include one thing the teacher said or did that surprised you.
hintStarters: I still remember the morning when…
While the other children were laughing, she…
sampleText: Her name was Mrs. Adeyemi, and she taught us English when I was eight years old in Lagos. I still remember one Tuesday morning. The classroom was hot, and the fan above her desk was making a loud noise. She asked a simple question, but I was too shy to answer. I knew the word, but my voice felt small. The other children were laughing at a joke and nobody noticed me. Then Mrs. Adeyemi walked between the desks and stopped next to my chair. She did not speak loudly. She bent down and said in a quiet voice, "You don't have to be perfect. You only have to try." Then she went back to the front of the class and asked the question again. This time I raised my hand. My answer was not great, but she smiled gently and wrote my name on the blackboard with a piece of yellow chalk. After class, she gave me a new notebook with a blue cover. I kept that notebook for many years. I was always afraid of making mistakes before that day. After that day, I was a little less afraid.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: quá nhút nhát để trả lời

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous + past simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past continuous cho bối cảnh đang diễn ra (was making, were laughing) và past simple cho sự việc xảy ra (walked, stopped, said).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past continuous for the background ('the fan was making a loud noise', 'the other children were laughing') and past simple for what happened ('walked', 'stopped', 'said').

sampleTranslationVi: Tên cô ấy là Mrs. Adeyemi, và cô đã dạy chúng tôi tiếng Anh khi tôi tám tuổi ở Lagos. Tôi vẫn nhớ một buổi sáng thứ Ba. Lớp học rất nóng và chiếc quạt phía trên bàn cô phát ra tiếng ồn lớn. Cô đặt một câu hỏi đơn giản, nhưng tôi quá nhút nhát để trả lời. Tôi biết từ đó, nhưng giọng tôi rất nhỏ. Những đứa trẻ khác đang cười vì một câu chuyện đùa và không ai để ý đến tôi. Rồi Mrs. Adeyemi đi giữa các dãy bàn và dừng lại cạnh ghế tôi. Cô không nói to. Cô cúi xuống và nói bằng giọng nhỏ nhẹ: "Em không cần phải hoàn hảo. Em chỉ cần cố gắng." Sau đó cô quay lại phía trước lớp và hỏi lại câu hỏi. Lần này tôi đã giơ tay. Câu trả lời của tôi không xuất sắc, nhưng cô mỉm cười nhẹ nhàng và viết tên tôi lên bảng đen bằng một viên phấn vàng. Sau giờ học, cô đưa tôi một cuốn vở mới bìa xanh. Tôi giữ cuốn vở đó nhiều năm. Trước ngày hôm đó tôi luôn sợ mắc lỗi. Sau ngày hôm đó, tôi bớt sợ hơn một chút.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best opening sentence for a memory like this.
options: Her name was Mrs. Adeyemi, and she taught us English when I was eight years old.
Her name Mrs. Adeyemi, she teaching us English when I am eight years old.
Her name has been Mrs. Adeyemi, and she has taught us English when I was eight.
Her name is Mrs. Adeyemi, and she is teaching us English when I am eight.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple ('was', 'taught') fits a finished childhood memory.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense do we use for a finished time in the past?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly mixes past continuous and past simple?
options: The fan above her desk was making a loud noise.
The fan above her desk make a loud noise.
The fan above her desk is making a loud noise.
The fan above her desk has been making a loud noise.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous ('was making') describes the background noise during the scene.
mcQuestions.hint: Background actions in a past story usually take past continuous.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural way to describe a soft, kind voice.
options: She said in a quiet voice.
She said in a little loud voice.
She said with quiet voice big.
She said by a not loud voice.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'In a quiet voice' is a natural collocation in English.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option sounds like a phrase a native speaker would use?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past form of 'raise'.
options: This time I raised my hand.
This time I rised my hand.
This time I rose my hand.
This time I raise my hand.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Raise' is a regular verb. Past simple is 'raised'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best uses 'because' to give a reason?
options: I didn't answer because I was too shy.
I didn't answer so I was too shy.
I didn't answer but I was too shy.
I didn't answer because I am too shy.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because' introduces a reason, and the rest of the story is in past tense.
mcQuestions.hint: Choose the linker that introduces a cause.

options: After class, she gave me a new notebook with a blue cover.
After class, she give me a new notebook with a blue cover.
After class, she has gave me a new notebook with a blue cover.
After the class, she giving me a new notebook with a blue cover.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past form of 'give'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural phrase for being scared of mistakes.
options: I was afraid of making mistakes.
I was afraid for to make mistakes.
I had afraid making mistakes.
I was afraid making mistake.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Be afraid of + -ing' is the standard pattern.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word best fills the gap? 'She ___ down and spoke to me.'
options: bent
bend
bending
has bent
mcQuestions.hint: What's the irregular past form of 'bend'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows a clear contrast?
options: My answer was not great, but she smiled gently.
My answer was not great, because she smiled gently.
My answer was not great, so she smiled gently.
My answer was not great, and she smiled gently.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'But' shows the contrast between a weak answer and a kind smile.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the best closing sentence for this kind of story.
options: After that day, I was a little less afraid.
After that day, I am a little less afraid.
After that day, I will be a little less afraid.
After that day, I have been a little less afraid yesterday.
mcQuestions.explanation: The whole story is in the past, so 'was' keeps the tense consistent.
mcQuestions.hint: Match the closing tense to the rest of the story.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Was there ever a small thing a teacher said to you that you still remember? Why do you think it stayed with you?
